content.problemStatement:
When there's no more room in hell, the dead will race the earth.

Spooky and NNN other ghosts are racing against each other. The racetrack is a straight line.

To make things interesting, the ghosts start at different positions.

The iii-th ghost starts AiA_iAi​ meters away from the finish line, and will drive at a constant speed of BiB_iBi​ units.

Unfortunately, the timer used by the ghosts can only report integers.

Specifically, if a racer finishes at time ttt, the timer reports their finish time to be ⌈t⌉\left\lceil t\right\rceil⌈t⌉ instead, where ⌈ ⌉\left\lceil \ \right\rceil⌈ ⌉ denotes the ceiling function.

Spooky hasn't decided where she'll start from, but she knows it won't be more than KKK meters away from the finish line. She also hasn't decided how fast she'll drive yet.

For each starting position D=1,2,3,…,KD = 1, 2, 3, \ldots, KD=1,2,3,…,K of Spooky, compute the following:


For a **non-empty** subset SSS of the NNN ghosts, let f(S)f(S)f(S) denote the minimum speed Spooky needs to drive at such that her reported time is **not strictly greater** than that of any of the ghosts in SSS, assuming she starts DDD meters away from the finish line.

Compute the expected value of f(S)f(S)f(S) across all **non-empty** subsets SSS of the NNN ghosts, modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
**Note:** It can be shown that the expectation can be written as a fraction PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP​ such that gcd⁡(P,Q)=1\gcd(P, Q) = 1gcd(P,Q)=1 and gcd⁡(Q,998244353)=1\gcd(Q, 998244353) = 1gcd(Q,998244353)=1.

There exists a unique integer RRR such that 0≤R<9982443530 \leq R \lt 9982443530≤R<998244353 and RQ≡P mod 998244353RQ\equiv P \bmod{998244353}RQ≡Pmod998244353.

Your task is to output RRR.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of three lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and KKK.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​.
The third line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​.




Output Format
For each test case, output a single line containing KKK space-separated integers. The iii-th of these should be the answer for D=iD = iD=i, modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 3⋅1053\cdot 10^53⋅105.
The sum of KKK over all test cases won't exceed 3⋅1053\cdot 10^53⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: The answers are as follows:

For D=1D = 1D=1 and D=2D = 2D=2, it's optimal for Spooky to drive at a speed of 111 no matter who the opponents are; so the expected minimum speed is also 111.
For D=4D = 4D=4, similarly it's optimal for Spooky to drive at a speed of 222 always.
For D=3D = 3D=3:

If only the first ghost is her opponent, Spooky can drive at a speed of 111 and not lose. There's a 13\frac{1}{3}31​ chance of this.
Otherwise, Spooky needs to drive at a speed of 222. There's a 23\frac{2}{3}32​ chance of this.
The expectation is 1⋅13+2⋅23=531\cdot\frac{1}{3} + 2\cdot\frac{2}{3} = \frac{5}{3}1⋅31​+2⋅32​=35​.


For D=5D = 5D=5:

If only the first ghost is her opponent, Spooky can drive at a speed of 222 and not lose. There's a 13\frac{1}{3}31​ chance of this.
Otherwise, Spooky needs to drive at a speed of 333. There's a 23\frac{2}{3}32​ chance of this.
The expectation is 2⋅13+3⋅23=832\cdot\frac{1}{3} + 3\cdot\frac{2}{3} = \frac{8}{3}2⋅31​+3⋅32​=38​.
